## honcho — recent conclusions

- Readaloud test/commit/deploy/verify flow: (1) Test with `direnv exec . mix compile --warnings-as-errors` from project root; for real tests use `direnv exec . env MIX_ENV=test mix test apps/<app>/test/<file>.exs` (umbrella-root mix test is brittle — per-app DBs need migrations; library/reader tests fail under per-app mix test because each spawns its own bare DB). (2) Commit — lefthook runs ast-grep, lint-grep, treefmt --fail-on-change; commit-msg uses conform conventional commits. Split mix-format-induced changes to unrelated files into a separate `chore: mix format` commit. (3) Deploy — push to GitHub master (configs flake input is `github:ngngardner/readaloud`), then in /home/noah/projects/configs run `/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/bin/nix flake update readaloud` (configs direnv shell does NOT include nix itself, must call full path), then `/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/bin/nix develop --command bash -c "deploy --on pylon"` (the `deploy` shell command wraps `colmena apply --impure --on <hosts>`). Watch for `Activation successful` and `All done!` from pylon — readaloud.service systemd unit gets restarted. (4) Verify — smoke with `curl -sS http://pylon:4000/`; for browser verification use Claude-in-Chrome MCP. Note: LiveView form_input and JS-dispatched change events do NOT reliably trigger phx-change handlers (form serialization fails outside a <form> tag); prefer finding a book already in the desired state, or read SQLite directly via `ssh root@pylon "sqlite3 /var/lib/readaloud/data/readaloud.db '<query>'"`. DB lives at /var/lib/readaloud/data/readaloud.db on pylon.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skillserver: Bridge between Claude Code and the MCP skillserver on pylon:4100. Use when the user says "/skillserver", "list skills", "list my skills", "search skills", "show skill", "run skill", "skillserver status", "what skills do I have", or invokes a skill by name that isn't locally installed (e.g. "/skillserver jason", "/skillserver good-morning"). Claude Code does not auto-discover skills hosted on the MCP skillserver — this skill bridges the gap. Do NOT use to create or edit skills (write files directly to `~/projects/configs/skills/` instead).

## Summary

I found the code responsible for the "Set up audio" popover and identified the issue with the VOICE dropdown:

### 1. **Main Component: BookLive (UI & Event Handlers)**
**File:** `/home/noah/projects/readaloud/apps/readaloud_web/lib/readaloud_web_web/live/book_live.ex`

- **Lines 243-289:** "Set up audio" button and popover dropdown (rendered when `!@book.audio_preferences`)
  - Line 245: "Set up audio" button text
  - Lines 252-265: MODEL dropdown using `phx-change="select_model"` handler
  - Lines 268-282: VOICE dropdown using `phx-change="select_voice"` handler
  - Line 284: "Activate" button calling `activate_audio`

- **Lines 107-111:** `handle_event("select_model", ...)` — updates `selected_model` and auto-sets voice to first voice of selected model
- **Lines 114-116:** `handle_event("select_voice", ...)` — updates `selected_voice`
- **Lines 49-75:** `handle_event("activate_audio", ...)` — saves selections to database
- **Lines 12, 31-32:** Loads models via `fetch_models()` and sets defaults

### 2. **Model/Voice Loading: LiveHelpers**
**File:** `/home/noah/projects/readaloud/apps/readaloud_web/lib/readaloud_web_web/live_helpers.ex`

- **Lines 14-19:** `fetch_models()` calls `ReadaloudTTS.list_models_and_voices()` to fetch model list
- **Lines 21-33:** `default_model()` and `default_voice()` initialize selections

### 3. **Voice Data Source: TTS Module**
**File:** `/home/noah/projects/readaloud/apps/readaloud_tts/lib/readaloud_tts/local_ai_provider.ex`

- **Lines 110-132:** `list_models_and_voices()` fetches models from LocalAI API (`/v1/models`), filters for TTS models, then maps each model to its voices from `Config.known_voices()`

**File:** `/home/noah/projects/readaloud/apps/readaloud_tts/lib/readaloud_tts/config.ex`

- **Lines 9-24:** `known_voices()` returns hardcoded voice mappings (currently only "kokoro" has voices defined: af_heart, af_nicole, etc.)

### 4. **The VOICE Dropdown Issue**
The VOICE dropdown is **not broken in code** — it's populated dynamically at **line 275-282** in book_live.ex:
```elixir
<% current_model = Enum.find(@models, &(&1[:id] == @selected_model)) %>
<option :for={v <- (current_model && current_model[:voices]) || []} ...>
```
It filters voices from the current model. **If it appears empty, likely because:**
- The model loaded doesn't exist in `known_voices()` in Config (line 9-21), OR
- The LocalAI API didn't return models, OR
- The model structure doesn't have a `:voices` key populated

### 5. **Tests**
No tests currently cover this behavior (no test files found for BookLive events or popover rendering).

defmodule ReadaloudWebWeb.LiveHelpers do
  @moduledoc "Shared helpers for all LiveViews."

  def active_task_count do
    import_count =
      ReadaloudImporter.list_tasks() |> Enum.count(&(&1.status in ["pending", "processing"]))

    audio_count =
      ReadaloudAudiobook.list_tasks() |> Enum.count(&(&1.status in ["pending", "processing"]))

    import_count + audio_count
  end

  def fetch_models do
    case ReadaloudTTS.list_models_and_voices() do
      {:ok, models} -> models
      {:error, _} -> []
    end
  end

  def default_model(book, models) do
    prefs = book.audio_preferences || %{}
    prefs["model"] || List.first(models)[:id] || ReadaloudTTS.Config.from_env().tts_model
  end

  def default_voice(book, models) do
    prefs = book.audio_preferences || %{}
    model_id = prefs["model"] || List.first(models)[:id]
    model = Enum.find(models, &(&1[:id] == model_id)) || %{}

    prefs["voice"] || get_in(model, [:voices]) |> List.wrap() |> List.first() ||
      ReadaloudTTS.Config.from_env().voice
  end
end

case Req.get("#{config.base_url}/v1/models") do
      {:ok, %{status: 200, body: %{"data" => models}}} ->
        {:ok, models}

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason}
    end
  end

  def list_models_and_voices(opts \\ []) do
    config = Keyword.get(opts, :config, Config.from_env())

    case Req.get("#{config.base_url}/v1/models") do
      {:ok, %{status: 200, body: %{"data" => models}}} ->
        tts_models =
          models
          |> Enum.filter(fn m -> String.contains?(m["id"] || "", ["tts", "kokoro", "piper"]) end)
          |> Enum.map(fn m ->
            model_id = m["id"]
            voices = Map.get(Config.known_voices(), model_id, [])
            %{id: model_id, voices: voices}
          end)

        {:ok, tts_models}

      {:ok, %{status: status}} ->
        {:error, "LocalAI returned #{status}"}

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason}
    end
  end
end

Now I understand the bug. The "Set up audio" popover has `<select phx-change="select_model">` and `<select phx-change="select_voice">` but they're NOT wrapped in a `<form>` tag. This is the same form-serialization issue the user already documented in memory. The user clicks "kokoro" in the model dropdown — the browser updates the visible `<select>` value, but `phx-change` outside a form fails to send the params, so server-side `@selected_model` stays at the initial value (`qwen3-tts-0.6b-custom-voice`), which has no voices in `Config.known_voices()`, so the voice dropdown stays empty.

The existing edit-mode popover (cog icon, lines 167-199) already uses a `<form>` wrapper — that pattern works. Let me apply the same fix to "Set up audio".

content.text:
The fix is in. Want me to commit & deploy, or test locally first? Note `apps/readaloud_web/lib/readaloud_web_web/live/reader_live.ex:498-507` has the exact same broken pattern (model/voice selects with `phx-change` outside a `<form>`) on the chapter reader's "Generate Audio" bar — same fix would apply. Should I include that in this commit?

Summary of the bug and fix:
- **Bug**: in `book_live.ex`, the "Set up audio" popover's `<select phx-change="select_model">` and `<select phx-change="select_voice">` were not wrapped in a `<form>` tag. LiveView form serialization fails outside `<form>`, so when the user picked "kokoro" the change never reached the server, `@selected_model` stayed at the default `qwen3-tts-0.6b-custom-voice` (which has no entries in `Config.known_voices/0`), and the voice dropdown rendered zero options.
- **Fix**: wrap the popover in `<form phx-change="update_audio_form" phx-submit="activate_audio">`, collapse `select_model`/`select_voice` into a single form-level handler that reconciles voice when model changes, and have `activate_audio` read from form params.

---
name: readaloud test/commit/deploy/verify flow
description: End-to-end procedure for landing a code change in readaloud — test, commit, deploy via configs flake input, verify in browser.
type: reference
originSessionId: 23193cc2-0713-490e-8c51-0225ef558d27
---
The readaloud project deploys to pylon as a NixOS module via flake input from GitHub. The full loop:

**1. Test**
- `direnv exec . mix compile --warnings-as-errors` from project root — fast sanity check.
- For real tests: `direnv exec . env MIX_ENV=test mix test apps/<app>/test/<file>.exs`. Umbrella-root `mix test` is brittle (per-app DBs need migrations) — prefer per-file invocation. The library/reader tests fail under per-app `mix test` because each spawns its own bare DB; ignore unless directly touched.
- Test DB needs migrations: `direnv exec . env MIX_ENV=test mix ecto.create && mix ecto.migrate`.
- `mix precommit` (alias) runs `compile --warnings-as-errors`, `deps.unlock --unused`, `format`, `test`. Note: format will reformat unrelated files — split those into a separate "chore: mix format" commit before the feature commit.

**2. Commit**
- Lefthook pre-commit runs ast-grep, lint-grep, treefmt (`--fail-on-change`). Treefmt scans all files, so any unformatted file blocks the commit.
- Commit-msg uses conform (conventional commits). Prefix with `feat(scope):`, `fix(scope):`, `chore:` etc. Keep header under ~70 chars.

**3. Deploy**
- Push to GitHub master: `git push origin master` (the configs flake input is `github:ngngardner/readaloud`).
- In `/home/noah/projects/configs/`: `/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/bin/nix flake update readaloud` to bump the lock.
  - Note: the configs project's direnv shell does NOT include `nix` itself — call `/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/bin/nix` directly.
- Deploy: `cd /home/noah/projects/configs && /nix/var/nix/profiles/default/bin/nix develop --command bash -c "deploy --on pylon"`. The `deploy` shell command wraps `colmena apply --impure --on <hosts>`.
- Watch for `Activation successful` and `All done!` from pylon. The `readaloud.service` systemd unit gets restarted in the `starting the following units:` line.

**4. Verify**
- Smoke: `curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://pylon:4000/` should return 200.
- Browser: use Claude-in-Chrome MCP. Point at `http://pylon:4000/`.
- For LiveView form interactions, `form_input` and JS-dispatched `change` events do NOT reliably trigger `phx-change` handlers (form serialization fails outside a `<form>` tag). For verifying state-dependent UI changes, prefer either: (a) finding a book that's already in the desired state, or (b) reading SQLite directly via `ssh root@pylon "sqlite3 /var/lib/readaloud/data/readaloud.db '<query>'"`.

**Useful queries:**
- Books with audio activated: `SELECT id, title, audio_preferences FROM books WHERE audio_preferences IS NOT NULL`
- DB path on pylon: `/var/lib/readaloud/data/readaloud.db`
- Service: `systemctl status readaloud` on pylon (root@pylon SSH).

input.new_string:
**4. Verify**
- Smoke: `curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://pylon:4000/` should return 200.
- Browser: use Claude-in-Chrome MCP against `http://pylon:4000/`.

**Browser verification recipe (Claude-in-Chrome MCP):**
1. `tabs_context_mcp createIfEmpty=true` once to get a `tabId` (and create a tab if none exists).
2. `browser_batch` for chained navigate + screenshot — saves round-trips.
3. To click DaisyUI dropdown triggers (`<div tabindex="0" role="button">`), use `find` to get a ref then `computer left_click ref=...`. Coordinate clicks on these can miss focus and the popover won't open.
4. To pick a `<select>` value, prefer `form_input` (works reliably **when the select is inside a `<form>` tag**). For phx-change selects rendered outside a form, form serialization is broken — but that's a code bug to fix (wrap in `<form phx-change=... phx-submit=...>`), not a test workaround.
5. `find query="..."` returns refs and (when the select is closed) lists its option text in the description — handy for asserting voice/model lists without opening the dropdown.

**Useful queries:**
- Books with audio activated: `SELECT id, title, audio_preferences FROM books WHERE audio_preferences IS NOT NULL`
- Books without audio (good for testing Set up audio): `SELECT id, title FROM books WHERE audio_preferences IS NULL LIMIT 5`
- DB path on pylon: `/var/lib/readaloud/data/readaloud.db`
- Service: `systemctl status readaloud` on pylon (root@pylon SSH).
- LocalAI URL the running service uses: `systemctl show readaloud.service --property=Environment | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -i localai`

lines: -- Browser: use Claude-in-Chrome MCP. Point at `http://pylon:4000/`.

-- For LiveView form interactions, `form_input` and JS-dispatched `change` events do NOT reliably trigger `phx-change` handlers (form serialization fails outside a `<form>` tag). For verifying state-dependent UI changes, prefer either: (a) finding a book that's already in the desired state, or (b) reading SQLite directly via `ssh root@pylon "sqlite3 /var/lib/readaloud/data/readaloud.db '<query>'"`.
+1. `tabs_context_mcp createIfEmpty=true` once to get a `tabId` (and create a tab if none exists).
+2. `browser_batch` for chained navigate + screenshot — saves round-trips.

+3. To click DaisyUI dropdown triggers (`<div tabindex="0" role="button">`), use `find` to get a ref then `computer left_click ref=...`. Coordinate clicks on these can miss focus and the popover won't open.

+4. To pick a `<select>` value, prefer `form_input` (works reliably **when the select is inside a `<form>` tag**). For phx-change selects rendered outside a form, form serialization is broken — but that's a code bug to fix (wrap in `<form phx-change=... phx-submit=...>`), not a test workaround.
+5. `find query="..."` returns refs and (when the select is closed) lists its option text in the description — handy for asserting voice/model lists without opening the dropdown.
- Books with audio activated: `SELECT id, title, audio_preferences FROM books WHERE audio_preferences IS NOT NULL`
+- Books without audio (good for testing Set up audio): `SELECT id, title FROM books WHERE audio_preferences IS NULL LIMIT 5`
- Service: `systemctl status readaloud` on pylon (root@pylon SSH).
+- LocalAI URL the running service uses: `systemctl show readaloud.service --property=Environment | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -i localai`
